english: v. rtóg-pa (rtog pa); as sbst., preceded by a genit., inquiry, examination, Stg., frq.; gen. c. accus. rmi-lam brtág(s)-pa (rmi lam brtag(s) pa) examination of dreams Stg.; rin-po-č'e brtág(s)-pa-la mk'ás-pa (rin po che brtag(s) pa la mkhas pa) connoisseur of precious stones Dzl.; brtágs-pa brgyad (brtags pa brgyad) Tar. 21, 2.?

a kind of imprecation, which consists in hiding the image and name of an enemy in the ground underneath an idol, and imploring the deity to kill him; brtad jùg-pa (brtad 'jug pa) to perform that ceremony Mil.
1. Lex. = bló-bur (blo bur) new, recent. - 2. Sch. haste, speed, for rtáb-pa (rtab pa) (?) (Tar. 180, 2 it should prob. be ytád-na (gtad na).)

adj. and abstract noun; brtán-po (brtan po) adj., firm, steadfast, safe; firmness etc.; brtán-par ynás-pa (brtan par gnas pa), *tán-po dád-ce* (*tan po dad ce*) W., to last, hold out, abide, continue, frq.; brtán-pa tób-pa (brtan pa thob pa) to become firm or durable (lit. to acquire firmness or durability) Mil.; brtán-par °gyúr-ba (brtan par gyur ba), *tán-po čó-če* (*tan po co ce*) W. id.; brtán-gyi skyid (brtan gyi skyid) a continued or abiding happiness Mil.; dbań brtan (dbang brtan) their strength is holding out Med.; brtán-du jùg-pa (brtan du 'jug pa) Glr., *tán-po čó-če* (*tan po co ce*) W., to watch, keep, preserve carefully; *tán-po kur* (*tan po khur*) W. carry it carefully or safely! dám-bcas-pa brtán-par šes (dam bcas pa brtan par shes) he knew his word to be inviolable Dzl.; yi-dam-la brtán-pas (yi dam la brtan pas) because he firmly kept his word Dzl.; dus brtán-gyi bdé-ba (dus brtan gyi bde ba) eternal welfare, everlasting happiness Mil. (perh. this ought to be ytan (gtan)).
, or bstán-ma (bstan ma), and bstán-pa-mo (bstan pa mo), n. of the goddess of the earth, (also skóń-ma (skong ma), yá-ma (ya ma)), used in practising magic.

1. deportment, behaviour Cs. - 2. Sch. also diligence, pains-taking(?). - brtul-żúgs (brtul zhugs), 1. Cs. manner, way of acting. 2. Sch. and gen.: exercise of penance, brtul-żúgs byéd-pa (brtul zhugs byed pa) or spyód-pa (spyod pa), to perform such exercises, to do penance. 3. penitent. - brtul-żúgs-čan (brtul zhugs can) penitent (adj. and sbst.) - brtul-p'ód-pa (brtul phod pa) v. rtul-p'ód-pa (rtul phod pa).

1. v. stón-pa (ston pa). - 2. sbst. doctrine, a single doctrine, or a whole system of doctrines; sańs-rgyás-kyi bstán-pa (sangs rgyas kyi bstan pa) the doctrine or religion of Buddha, tub-bstán (thub bstan), for túb-pai bstán-pa (thub pa'i bstan pa), id.; ynás-lugs bstán-pa (gnas lugs bstan pa) the doctrine of the position of... Med.; bstán-pa ynyis (bstan pa gnyis) with Urgyan Padma etc., the same as mdoi (mdo'i) and sńágs-kyi lam (sngags kyi lam), v. mdo (mdo) extr. - bstán-°gyur (bstan 'gyur) the second great literary production of Buddhism, containing comments on Kan-°gyur (bka' 'gyur), and scientific treatises (v. bka-°gyur (bka' 'gyur) in bka (bka')) Köpp. II, 280. - bstan-bcòs (bstan bcos) (शास्त्र) a scientific work. - bstan-rtsis (bstan rtsis) a chronological work relative to the year of Buddha's death. - bstan-°dzin (bstan 'dzin) follower, adherent of a doctrine, sańs-rgyás-kyi bstan-°dzin (sangs rgyas kyi bstan 'dzin) Mil., Buddhist; also frq. used as a noun personal. - bstan-(b)šig (bstan (b)shig) col. a destroyer of the doctrine, in general a good-for-nothing fellow, a mischief-maker, an obnoxious person or thing. - bstan-srúń (bstan srung) 1. a keeper, guardian of the doctrine; perh. also = bstan-°dzin (bstan 'dzin). 2. keeper, warden, guardian in general, lha-k'áń-gi bstan-srúń (lha khang gi bstan srung); lhá-sai bstan-srúń (lha sa'i bstan srung) the tutelar goddess of Lhasa, acc. to Glr. = dpal-lhá-mo (dpal lha mo). 3. in general the contrary to bstan-bšig (bstan bshig).
supine of sti-ba (sti ba); bstir-méd (bstir med) 'restlessness', one of the infernal regions.
to make lower, to lower Sch. (?).
1. vb. v. stén-pa (sten pa). 2. sbst. confidence, = brtón-pa (brton pa) Bhar.
